data.title: United Reaches Tentative Agreement with Flight Attendant Union
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

United Airlines reached a tentative agreement with its flight attendants' union for wages and scheduling improvements.

If the agreement with the Association of Flight Attendants-CWA is ratified, it will implement immediate raises and wage rates that reach $100 per hour by the end of the agreement.

It also includes boarding pay, new pay for long gaps between flights and a signing bonus for every flight attendant, the airline said Thursday. The total bonus package would be valued at $740 million.

The tentative agreement is subject to approval by the union's master executive council, including all local presidents. If they approve, the agreement will be put out for ratification by United's flight attendants.

If ratified, the new contract would become amendable after five years.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

  • The maker of Absolut vodka and Martell cognac has held some initial talks over the combination, Bloomberg reports.

data.title: Bank of Canada to Protect Economy From Risk of Persistent Inflation, No. 2 Official Says — Update
data.description:
By Paul Vieira

OTTAWA--The Bank of Canada's priority is to ensure that higher energy prices stemming from the war in Iran do not morph into an extended period of elevated inflation, the central bank's No. 2 official said.

Officials expect the recent increase in crude-oil and natural-gas costs to lead to higher inflation in the coming months, Senior Deputy Gov. Carolyn Rogers said. Headline inflation in Canada dropped below 2% in February, prior to the sharp jump in energy prices.

"What we need to guard against is that higher energy prices start to spread to other goods and services and become ongoing, persistent inflation," said Rogers, according to prepared remarks she was set to deliver Thursday in Brandon, Manitoba.

The Bank of Canada's mandate is to set interest-rate policy to achieve and maintain 2% inflation.

Some economists were expecting that Rogers's speech might try to either rebut or buttress market expectations for three Bank of Canada interest-rate increases before the end of 2026 to deal with the fallout from higher fuel costs. Sovereign-bond yields, including Canada's, have climbed as traders anticipate that higher inflation is in the offing.

Rogers's remarks stuck closely to language used by the central bank last week in its decision to keep the target for the overnight rate unchanged at 2.25%. At that time, Gov. Tiff Macklem said the risk of higher energy costs spreading and lifting prices for other goods and services looked contained at present, citing excess spare capacity floating around in the economy. He added, however, officials wouldn't let the effect of higher energy prices broaden and become persistent inflation.

Rogers said higher energy prices would lift Canadian income, reflecting Canada's position as a net exporter of crude and natural gas. However, she added that the rapid rise in fuel costs would squeeze household budgets and corporate margins.

"Tighter financial conditions and yet more uncertainty will also weigh on spending and investment," said Rogers, in reference to jump in bond yields, which lenders use to help set interest rates on consumer and business loans.

She said officials would be monitoring developments in the Middle East and assessing the effect on growth and inflation. "As the outlook evolves, we stand ready to respond as needed," Rogers said.

Some economists argue that the higher fuel costs would be partly offset by the underlying weakness in the Canadian economy, due to strains from U.S. trade policy and a curb on immigration growth. For that reason, some economists don't expect the Bank of Canada to raise rates in 2026.

Write to Paul Vieira at paul.vieira@wsj.com

data.title: Memory Stock Selloff Seen as Healthy Rebalance — Market Talk
data.description: Investors are selling off U.S. memory stocks to price in concerns about long-term strength, Morgan Stanley analysts say. Shareholders feel positively about growth in the short-term, as memory shortages are driving up prices, but there is low conviction that growth will continue at this pace further down the road, the analysts say. Sandisk is off 9%, while Micron Technology is down 6%. The analysts say this is a healthy selloff given gains in recent months, but they think demand will be enough to spur long-term growth. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

data.title: Best Buy's stock is rallying amid takeover speculation. But here's the real story.
data.description:
By Bill Peters

Some attributed the jump in Best Buy's stock to the prospect of a takeover by GameStop, but the analyst who wrote the note that started that speculation says otherwise

Shares of Best Buy are surging amid what one analyst says is unwarranted takeover speculation.

Shares of electronics chain Best Buy jumped on Thursday to become one of the S&P 500's strongest performers, a move some on social media attributed to a potential takeover by videogame retailer and meme-stock name GameStop.

But the analyst research note released Thursday that initially fueled that speculation, from the firm Gordon Haskett, actually said it was still unclear what company GameStop (GME) might have its eye on. And it said recent efforts by GameStop to put its money to work toward a transaction of some kind didn't appear to signal interest in Best Buy (BBY) for now.

Shares of Best Buy were up 5.1% in recent afternoon trading on Thursday but were still down 15.2% over the past 12 months. GameStop's stock fell 2% on Thursday and has dropped 20.3% over the past year.

Best Buy and GameStop did not immediately respond to requests for comment.

In January, GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen told CNBC that the company intended to make a "very, very big" acquisition of a publicly traded company in the consumer space. In Thursday's note, Gordon Haskett analyst Don Bilson said Cohen had secured a large incentive package weeks before.

Because of the way the compensation package is structured, Bilson believes Cohen has an incentive to "swing for the fences" and buy a company that already generates a lot of earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization, a measure of underlying profitability.

"Far be it for us to know who Cohen is after but it does look like he has put the wheels in motion," Bilson wrote.

He noted that a filing on Tuesday from GameStop (GME), made in conjunction with its quarterly earnings, said that after GameStop closed out its fiscal year, which ran through January, the company put around $700 million in cash into a company account that "is pledged as collateral for certain existing and potential cash or physically settled derivative transactions."

He said that line from the filing indicated that GameStop may have bought a position on swap, and that Cohen likely has a "very substantial economic exposure" to a particular name.

"The billion dollar question is what name that might be," Bilson wrote. "Previously, we have thrown Best Buy's name against the wall and we did see some prime broker action in that name during [the fourth quarter] but that doesn't square with [GameStop] saying it put its money to work after its fiscal year ended."

He believes that whatever Cohen may have bought "should stand out like a sore thumb" when the counterparty discloses its equity holdings in mid-May or discloses a 5% position in a company.

GameStop has faced falling sales in recent years amid a shift toward online gaming. Best Buy's stock put up gains earlier this month after the company's quarterly results, which cleared low expectations from Wall Street. Gaming sales growth slowed, but gains were higher in artificial-intelligence glasses and 3-D printers.

The 20.3% drop in GameStop's stock over the past year comes as the State Street SPDR S&P Retail ETF XRT has rallied 13.8% and the S&P 500 index SPX has gained14.1% over the same period.

-Bill Peters

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Belgium's Exmar posts lower profit as Congo contract winds down, Bexco sale weighs
data.description:
Belgian gas shipping and infrastructure group Exmar posted an annual adjusted operating profit of $101.6 million, around 4% lower than a year earlier, dragged down by the completion of a major LNG construction contract in Congo and the disposal of subsidiary Bexco.

Exmar, which provides floating infrastructure solutions to the oil and gas industry, said it saw no immediate operational impact resulting from the war in Iran and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

  • Annual revenue plunged 29% to $248.1 million, largely due to the completion of the Marine XII EPC contract in Congo and the exit of Bexco from the group's consolidation scope.

  • Infrastructure EBITDA slumped to $80.9 million from $143.6 million as the Congo contract wound down, while supporting services swung to a $7.9 million loss, hit by the Bexco sale and a stronger euro.

  • The shipping segment remained stable year-on-year, with solid contract cover and firm LPG spot rates offsetting a weaker second quarter.

  • The board proposes an annual dividend of 0.27 euro per share.

(1 euro = $1.1535)

data.title: Portugal's Catholic Church to pay $1.85 million to 57 victims of sexual abuse
data.description:
Portugal's Catholic Church said on Thursday it would pay a combined 1.6 million euros ($1.85 million) in compensation to 57 victims of sexual abuse by the clergy.

A Church-funded commission found in February 2023 that at least 4,815 children and vulnerable adults in Portugal were sexually abused by the Catholic clergy, mostly priests, over the past 70 years.

"Financial compensation does not erase what happened or undo the consequences of abuse in the lives of those affected... We renew our apology for all the harm caused," the Portuguese Bishops' Conference (CEP) said in a statement.

CEP said only 95 people requested compensation during the submission period, with 67 eligible and the remainder dismissed for failing to meet requirements or attend the commission.

The CEP said 57 compensation claims had already been approved, with payments ranging from 9,000 euros to 45,000 euros per person.

Nine other claims are still undergoing final review to determine the amount of compensation, it said.

($1 = 0.8666 euros)

data.title: Belgium's Campine 2025 revenue doubles on strength in antimony market
data.description:
Overview

  • Belgium specialty chemicals and metals recycler's 2025 revenue more than doubled on strong antimony market

  • EBITDA more than doubled to record €89 mln, aided by higher antimony prices and Ecobat acquisition

  • Company acquired Ecobat’s European battery recycling, boosting circular metals business

Outlook

  • Campine expects mid-year 2026 EBITDA around €40 mln

  • Campine expects further efficiency improvements at newly acquired Ecobat plants in 2026

Result Drivers

  • ANTIMONY MARKET VOLATILITY - Surge in antimony metal prices and exit of Chinese suppliers drove revenue and EBITDA growth, though demand softened late in year as prices fell

  • ECOBAT ACQUISITION - Strategic acquisition of Ecobat’s European battery recycling operations expanded circular metals segment and contributed to EBITDA

  • PLASTICS RECYCLING INTEGRATION - Integrated sourcing from battery recycling operations boosted plastics recycling sales volumes despite weak market conditions

data.title: Syensqo notifies bondholders of the Redemption Price for its €500 million senior bonds
data.description:
Press release

Regulated information

Syensqo notifies bondholders of the Redemption Price for its €500 million senior bonds

Brussels, March 26, 2026 - 6.15 pm CET

Following the publication by the Issuer on 27 February 2026 of a notice announcing Syensqo's decision to redeem all of the outstanding 2.750 per cent. Fixed Rate Bonds due December 2, 2027 (the “Bonds”) on Tuesday 31 March 2026, the company today announced the total redemption price. The Bonds will be redeemed at an aggregate redemption price equal to the principal amount of 500,000,000.00 € plus accrued interest of 4,482,900.00 €.

In accordance with the Conditions of the Bonds, KBC Bank NV, in its capacity as Calculation Agent, has today determined the Reference Rate for the redemption (3.062%). KBC Bank NV has also computed the Make-whole Amount (100,000.00 € per 100,000 € Bond) and the Interest accrued on the Bonds to, but excluding, the Make-whole Redemption Date (896.58 € per 100,000 € Bond). The total redemption price (the “Redemption Price”) is therefore 100,896.58 € per €100,000 Bond.

Link to the notice published on the Luxembourg Stock Exchange.

Full press release available here.

About Syensqo

Syensqo is a science company developing groundbreaking solutions that enhance the way we live, work, travel and play. Inspired by the scientific councils which Ernest Solvay initiated in 1911, we bring great minds together to push the limits of science and innovation for the benefit of our customers, with a diverse, global team of more than 13,000 associates in 30 countries.

Our solutions contribute to safer, cleaner, and more sustainable products found in homes, food and consumer goods, planes, cars, batteries, smart devices and healthcare applications. Our innovation power enables us to deliver on the ambition of a circular economy and explore breakthrough technologies that advance humanity.

Learn more at www.syensqo.com.

Contacts

  • Media Relationsmedia.relations@syensqo.com Perrine Marchal+32 478 32 62 72Laetitia Schreiber+32 487 74 38 07
  • Investors & Analystsinvestor.relations@syensqo.com Sherief Bakr+44 7920 575 989Robbin Moore-Randolph+1 470 493 2433Loïc Flament+32 478 69 74 20Eva Behaeghe+32 474 49 23 50

Safe harbor

This press release may contain forward-looking information. Forward-looking statements describe expectations, plans, strategies, goals, future events or intentions. The achievement of forward-looking statements contained in this press release is subject to risks and uncertainties relating to a number of factors, including general economic factors, interest rate and foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, changing market conditions, product competition, the nature of product development, impact of acquisitions and divestitures, restructurings, products withdrawals, regulatory approval processes, all-in scenario of R&I projects and other unusual items. Consequently, actual results or future events may differ materially from those expressed or implied by such forward-looking statements. Should known or unknown risks or uncertainties materialize, or should our assumptions prove inaccurate, actual results could vary materially from those anticipated. The Company undertakes no obligation to publicly update or revise any forward-looking statements.

data.title: Linkhome Holdings 2025 Form 10-K: Revenue $21.0M, EPS $0.00
data.description:
Linkhome Holdings reported $21.0 million in net revenues for 2025, driven by a shift to Cash Offer transactions, while net income was $74,874 and basic and diluted earnings per share were $0.00 on 15,216,699 weighted average common shares. Revenue grew sharply year-over-year, but gross profit and net income declined relative to the prior year due to compressing margins and higher operating expenses. The company is scaling its platform and fintech capabilities to support increased Cash Offer transaction volume.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Revenues: $21.0 million, increase of $13.38 million (175.69%) versus 2024 driven by Cash Offer transactions.
  • Gross Profit: $0.77 million; gross margin 3.68%, down from $1.47 million (19.31%) in 2024 due to lower Cash Offer margins.
  • Operating Income: $76,432, a decline from $1,089,420 in 2024 reflecting higher selling, general and administrative expenses (G&A up 81.39%).
  • Net Income: $74,874, down 90.38% versus prior year due to lower gross profit despite revenue growth.
  • Earnings per Share – Basic and Diluted: $0.00; weighted average common shares outstanding: 15,216,699.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth and Channel Shift: Net revenues rose 175.7% year-over-year to $21.0 million in 2025 as the Cash Offer program became the primary channel, accounting for 96% of revenue and increasing transactions from 6 to 20 deals.
  • Platform and AI Strategy: Ongoing development of an AI-enabled real estate platform aimed at streamlining search, matching, transaction coordination and fintech features to improve conversion and scale operations.
  • Operational Scaling: Company expanded marketing efforts, opened a new office lease, increased hiring, and capitalized software development to support platform expansion and higher transaction throughput.
  • Fintech Product Momentum: The Cash Offer fintech enabled temporary property acquisitions to secure all-cash offers, enhancing client acquisition and conversion rates and forming a central part of go-to-market strategy.
  • Outlook (Operational): Continued focus on scaling the AI-enabled platform and fintech capabilities to support increased transaction volume and market competitiveness.

Original SEC Filing: Linkhome Holdings Inc. [ LHAI ] - 10-K - Mar. 26, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: UK's FTSE 100 falls more than 1% on Middle East uncertainty; 3i shares slide
data.description:
London's FTSE 100 equities index dropped more than 1% on Thursday, as lingering uncertainty over a Middle East ceasefire weighed on sentiment, while a steep fall in 3i Group shares added further pressure.

A senior Iranian official told Reuters the U.S. proposal was "one‑sided and unfair", while U.S. President Donald Trump warned that Iranian negotiators "better get serious soon" or there would be "NO TURNING BACK."

The blue-chip FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 closed down 1.3%, snapping a two‑day winning streak, while the midcap FTSE 250 index fell 0.8%.

  • 3i Group shares shed 17.6% to more than a two-year low after the private equity firm said its discount retailer Action expects like-for-like sales growth between 4% and 5% in 2026, broadly similar to the 4.9% growth it recorded in 2025.

  • Global stocks slipped on Thursday as the price of Brent crude pushed past $105 a barrel. That move followed the Iranian government's announcement that it was ruling out talks with Washington, dampening expectations of a quick end to the nearly month‑long U.S.-Israeli war with Iran.

  • Around 90% of the economists polled by Reuters now expect the Bank of England to hold its key interest rate at 3.75% through the end of this year, even as markets price in nearly two or three hikes after war‑driven energy shocks forced a sharp reversal from earlier this year, when investors were expecting rate cuts.

  • Britain's economic growth prospects this year received the sharpest downgrade of any major economy in the OECD's interim forecast update following the Middle East conflict.

  • Next shares rose 4.2% to the top of the benchmark after CEO Simon Wolfson said the clothing retailer has not seen a noticeable drop-off in UK sales since the war started at the end of February.

  • Playtech shares fell 12.1%, pushing the stock to the bottom of the midcap index, after the online gambling platform missed revenue estimates for its 2025 fiscal year.

data.title: FTC Warns Payment Processors Against 'Debanking'
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

The Federal Trade Commission warned four leading payment processors against denying customers access to financial services due to their political or religious views.

The letters, sent by FTC Chair Andrew Ferguson to the chief executives of Mastercard, Visa, PayPal and Stripe, cited President Trump's August executive order on "debanking," which Ferguson said "makes clear that it is unacceptable to debank law-abiding citizens due to 'political affiliations, religious beliefs, or lawful business activities.'"

Ferguson wrote that deplatforming of customers or denial of access to services "that is inconsistent with their terms of service or a customer's reasonable expectations" could lead to an FTC investigation and potential enforcement actions for violations of the FTC Act.

"Full participation in commerce and public life necessarily requires that law-abiding individuals can access, and freely participate in, our financial system," he wrote.

Representatives for the four companies didn't immediately respond to requests for comment.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Hungary government accuses journalist of spying for Ukraine
data.description:
Hungarian authorities said on Thursday they had launched an investigation into a journalist over accusations that he was spying for Ukraine amid an increasingly acrimonious election campaign - allegations dismissed by the reporter.

Nationalist Prime Minister Viktor Orban, who has kept close ties with Russia during Moscow's invasion of Ukraine, is fighting to extend his 16 years in office in the face of an unprecedented challenge from a centre-right opposition party.

On Thursday, Orban's chief of staff told a briefing that journalist Szabolcs Panyi, who has been investigating the government's ties to Moscow, was a spy working for Ukraine.

"More and more Ukrainian spies are uncovered in the country. The first of them is Szabolcs Panyi, who was discovered ... to have spied against his home country in collusion with a foreign state," Gergely Gulyas said.

Government spokesperson Zoltan Kovacs said a formal investigation has been launched into the journalist. Police did not immediately respond to emailed questions from Reuters.

Panyi dismissed the accusation.

"Accusing investigative journalists of espionage is entirely unprecedented in the 21st century from a European Union member state," Panyi wrote on his Facebook page.

"This is truly characteristic of Putin's Russia, Belarus and similar regimes."

Panyi is an investigative journalist who works for the Hungarian non-profit investigative outlet Direkt36 and Warsaw-based Vsquare.org.

As campaigning has accelerated in the build-up to the April 12 parliamentary election, relations between Budapest and Kyiv have reached new lows.

Orban has cast the election as a stark choice between "war or peace", saying his centre-right opponent would drag Hungary into the war raging next door in Ukraine, an allegation the opposition has firmly denied.

He also repeated accusations that Kyiv and Brussels are interfering in Hungary's election, a charge both reject.

On Monday, Orban ordered an investigation into what he said was the wiretapping of his foreign minister, Peter Szijjarto, as his government responded to a media report about its links to Russia.
